2604.14766.title: Temporal Cross-Modal Knowledge-Distillation-Based Transfer-Learning for Gas Turbine Vibration Fault Detection
2604.14766.paper_summary: Preventing machine failure is inherently superior to reactive remediation, particularly for critical assets like gas turbines, where early fault detection (FD) is a cornerstone of industrial sustainability. However, modern deep learning-based FD models often face a significant trade-off between architectural complexity and real-time operational constraints, often hindered by a lack of temporal context within restricted vibration signal windows. To address these challenges, this study proposes a Temporal Cross-Modal Knowledge-Distillation Transfer-Learning (TCMKDTL) framework. The framework employs a "privileged" teacher model trained on expansive temporal windows incorporating both past and future signal context to distill latent feature-based knowledge into a compact student model. To mitigate issues of data scarcity and domain shift, the framework leverages robust pre-training on benchmark datasets (such as CWRU) followed by adaptation to target industrial data. Extensive evaluation using experimental and industrial gas turbine (MGT-40) datasets demonstrates that TCMKDTL achieves superior feature separability and diagnostic accuracy compared to conventional pre-trained architectures. Ultimately, this approach enables high-performance, unsupervised anomaly detection suitable for deployment on resource-constrained industrial hardware.
2604.14766.paper_summary_zh: 防止機器故障本質上優於反應式修復，尤其是對於像燃氣渦輪這樣的關鍵資產，其中早期故障檢測（FD）是工業可持續性的基石。<lb/>然而，現代基於深度學習的FD模型往往面臨架構複雜性與實時操作限制之間的重大權衡，通常因為在有限的振動信號窗口中缺乏時間上下文而受到阻礙。<lb/>為了解決這些挑戰，本研究提出了一個時間跨模態知識蒸餾轉移學習（TCMKDTL）框架。<lb/>該框架採用一個“特權”教師模型，該模型在包含過去和未來信號上下文的廣泛時間窗口上進行訓練，以將潛在特徵基礎的知識蒸餾到一個緊湊的學生模型中。<lb/>為了減輕數據稀缺和領域轉移的問題，該框架利用在基準數據集（如CWRU）上的穩健預訓練，然後適應目標工業數據。<lb/>使用實驗和工業燃氣渦輪（MGT-40）數據集進行的廣泛評估表明，TCMKDTL在特徵可分離性和診斷準確性方面優於傳統的預訓練架構。<lb/>最終，這種方法實現了高性能的無監督異常檢測，適合在資源受限的工業硬體上部署。
2604.14766.author: Ali Bagheri Nejad et.al.
2604.14766.authors: Ali Bagheri Nejad, Mahdi Aliyari-Shoorehdeli, Abolfazl Hasanzadeh
